assertion_results.text: The changelog identifies the release as a minor version bump (v1.9.0) because the highest-impact commits are features
assertion_results.evidence: Header reads '## [1.9.0]' and a note states 'This is a minor release — the highest-impact changes are new features.'

assertion_results.text: Commits are grouped under category headings (Features, Bug Fixes, Performance, Dependencies) rather than a flat bullet list
assertion_results.evidence: Output has ### Features, ### Bug Fixes, ### Performance, ### Dependencies headings with commits grouped beneath each

assertion_results.text: All three feature commits appear under a Features heading with their scope and PR links
assertion_results.evidence: Features section lists: client: add retry logic (#312), streaming: SSE support (#318), types: export TypeScript definitions (#320)

assertion_results.text: All five bug fix commits appear under a Bug Fixes heading with scope, description, and PR links
assertion_results.evidence: Bug Fixes section lists all 5: auth expired tokens (#305), auth double refresh (#308), pagination off-by-one (#311), websocket reconnect (#314), cache TTL (#316)

assertion_results.text: Both performance commits appear under a Performance heading
assertion_results.evidence: Performance section lists: serialization: streaming JSON parser (#309), connection: HTTP/2 pooling (#313)

assertion_results.text: Dependency bumps are grouped under a Dependencies heading showing old and new versions
assertion_results.evidence: Dependencies section shows 'Bump axios from 1.6.2 to 1.7.0' and 'Bump typescript from 5.3.2 to 5.4.0'

assertion_results.text: PR links are formatted as clickable Markdown links pointing to https://github.com/acme/node-sdk/pull/NNN
assertion_results.evidence: PR numbers are shown as (#312) but not formatted as clickable links. Missing [#312](https://github.com/acme/node-sdk/pull/312) format.

assertion_results.text: A compare link is included showing the full diff between v1.8.0 and v1.9.0
